texto: When love and hate are both absent, everything becomes clear and undisguised. Make the slightest distinction, however, and heaven and earth are set infinitely apart. If you wish to see the truth, then hold no opinions for or against anything. To set up what you like against what you dislike is the disease of the mind.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hsin Hsin Ming: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “When love and hate are both absent, everything becomes clear and undisguised. Make the slightest …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, buddhism, christianity pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de christianity e hate.
